title: Las Vegas 5-Day Nightlife Getaway
metaTitle: Las Vegas 5-Day Nightlife Getaway | Flights, Hotels & Tours Included
metaDescription: Book a 5-day trip to Las Vegas, United States: round-trip flights, hotel, breakfast, transfers, and guided tours including High Roller Observation Wheel and The Sphere show. Prices from $1140 per person (estimate).
highlights: Red Rock Canyon rock climbing and hiking
High-roller suite at The Wynn or Bellagio
overview: Spend 5 days discovering Las Vegas, United States — Hot desert climate with scorching, dry summers (Jun-Sep, often above 40°C) and mild winters (Dec-Feb) with cool nights; humidity and rainfall are low year-round. March to May and September to November for warm days without the extreme summer desert heat. This itinerary blends High Roller Observation Wheel, The Sphere show, Fountains of Bellagio with free time to explore at your own pace, backed by round-trip flights, handpicked accommodation, and a dedicated travel consultant from enquiry through departure.
included: Round-trip economy-class airfare from your selected origin city (upgrade to premium economy or business available)
4 nights accommodation in your selected category (Budget, Mid-range, Premium, or Luxury)
Daily local transport as per the itinerary
Guided tours including High Roller Observation Wheel, The Sphere show, Fountains of Bellagio
All entrance fees for attractions and activities listed in the itinerary
Applicable taxes and OTA service fee
Dedicated travel consultant support from booking through departure
notIncluded: Visa fees and travel document costs (see Visa Information)
Travel insurance (strongly recommended, available as an add-on)
Lunches and dinners except where noted in the itinerary
Optional activities not listed in the day-by-day itinerary
Airline seat selection and baggage upgrades
Any fare difference for premium economy or business class upgrades

itinerary.title: Arrive in Las Vegas
itinerary.description: Land at Harry Reid International Airport (LAS) and take your included private transfer to your hotel. After settling in, ease into the trip with a relaxed visit to High Roller Observation Wheel — 550-foot Strip observation wheel. Round off the evening with a welcome dinner introducing you to local flavors.

itinerary.title: Las Vegas: The Sphere show & Neon Museum
itinerary.description: Morning visit to The Sphere show — Immersive show inside the iconic Sphere venue. In the afternoon, continue to Neon Museum — Collection of retired historic casino signage. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Las Vegas: Mob Museum & Eiffel Tower Viewing Deck (Paris Las Vegas)
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Mob Museum — Organized crime and law enforcement history museum. In the afternoon, continue to Eiffel Tower Viewing Deck (Paris Las Vegas) — Half-scale replica observation deck. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.
activities: Eiffel Tower Viewing Deck (Paris Las Vegas)

itinerary.title: Day Trip to Hoover Dam
itinerary.description: Full-day excursion to Hoover Dam, a popular side trip from Las Vegas. Return to Las Vegas in the evening with time free to explore on your own.

itinerary.title: Departure Day
itinerary.description: Enjoy a final morning at leisure — perhaps one last visit to Titanic Exhibition (Luxor) or some souvenir shopping — before your private transfer to LAS for your departure flight.

travelTips: Book shows and restaurant reservations in advance, especially for headliner residencies
Budget extra for mandatory resort fees added to hotel bills
Set a gambling budget beforehand and treat any winnings as a bonus
Use the pedestrian bridges rather than crossing the Strip at street level
Pace yourself — this itinerary includes free time so you are not rushing between every attraction.
packingList: Copies of booking confirmations and travel insurance
Lightweight, breathable clothing and a reusable water bottle
Sunscreen and a hat for intense desert sun
Comfortable shoes for walking long casino resort distances
A light jacket for cool desert nights and heavily air-conditioned interiors
Smart-casual outfits for nightclub and fine-dining dress codes
thingsToDo: Eiffel Tower Viewing Deck (Paris Las Vegas)
Red Rock Canyon scenic drive entry
Welcome to Fabulous Las Vegas sign
Red Rock Canyon rock climbing and hiking
thingsToAvoid: Accepting flyers or "free" show tickets from Strip promoters, which often lead to timeshare pitches
Underestimating resort fees and parking charges not shown in the room rate
Walking the entire Strip in flip-flops in summer heat
Drinking too much without hydrating in the dry desert air
topRestaurants: Gordon Ramsay Hell's Kitchen — Modern American ($$$)
Hash House A Go Go — American comfort food ($$)
Peppermill Restaurant — American diner and lounge ($$)
instagramSpots: Welcome to Fabulous Las Vegas sign
adventureActivities: Red Rock Canyon rock climbing and hiking
luxuryExperiences: High-roller suite at The Wynn or Bellagio
Private helicopter tour of the Strip at night
VIP table service at Omnia Nightclub
honeymoonExperiences: Sunset dinner at the Eiffel Tower Restaurant
Couples spa day at a Strip resort
Romantic gondola ride at The Venetian
budgetExperiences: Fremont Street Experience nightly light show
Browsing casino interiors and architecture for free
localEtiquette: Tipping is expected everywhere — dealers, bartenders, valet, and housekeeping (15-20%).
Smoking is still permitted on many casino floors.
Dress codes apply at upscale nightclubs and restaurants.
The gambling age is strictly 21+, enforced with ID checks.
Photography is often restricted inside casinos.
localTransportGuide: The Las Vegas Monorail connects several Strip resorts quickly, and the Strip itself is walkable via pedestrian bridges, though distances between casino entrances can be deceptively long. Uber, Lyft, and taxis are the most reliable way to reach Downtown, the airport, or day-trip departure points.
bestTimeToVisit: March to May and September to November for warm days without the extreme summer desert heat.
weather: Hot desert climate with scorching, dry summers (Jun-Sep, often above 40°C) and mild winters (Dec-Feb) with cool nights; humidity and rainfall are low year-round.
visaInformation: Travelers from Visa Waiver Program countries need an approved ESTA before flying, valid for stays up to 90 days; other nationalities require a B-2 tourist visa.
averageFlightTime: ~15.1h from major hubs (estimate; varies by origin — see Suggested Flights)
cancellationPolicy: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.
termsAndConditions: Pricing shown is per person based on the traveler type and tier selected and is an estimate pending live fare and rate confirmation.
Passports must be valid for at least 6 months beyond the return date for most destinations — confirm the exact requirement for your nationality.
Itinerary order may change due to weather, local conditions, or attraction closures; inclusions remain equivalent.
Solo travelers incur a single-room supplement, reflected in the Solo pricing tier.
This package does not include international travel insurance unless explicitly listed under Included.
Prices shown in a currency other than USD are approximate conversions at the rate noted and are not a payment quote.

faqs.question: Do I need a visa to visit Las Vegas?
faqs.answer: Travelers from Visa Waiver Program countries need an approved ESTA before flying, valid for stays up to 90 days; other nationalities require a B-2 tourist visa.

faqs.question: What is the best time to visit Las Vegas?
faqs.answer: March to May and September to November for warm days without the extreme summer desert heat. Hot desert climate with scorching, dry summers (Jun-Sep, often above 40°C) and mild winters (Dec-Feb) with cool nights; humidity and rainfall are low year-round.

faqs.question: What's included in this package?
faqs.answer: Round-trip airfare, accommodation, daily breakfast, airport transfers, daily local transport, and guided tours to the attractions listed in the itinerary. See the Included / Not Included sections for full detail.

faqs.question: Can the itinerary be customized?
faqs.answer: Yes — this itinerary is a starting point. Your travel consultant can add, remove, or reorder activities, extend your stay, or upgrade your hotel category before you book.

faqs.question: What is the cancellation policy?
faqs.answer: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.

faqs.question: Is Las Vegas safe for tourists?
faqs.answer: Las Vegas is generally safe for tourists who take standard precautions — avoid poorly lit areas at night, keep valuables secure, and use licensed transport.

faqs.question: What currency should I bring to Las Vegas?
faqs.answer: The local currency is USD. Cards and mobile payments are widely accepted, and ATMs are easy to find.

faqs.question: Is this package good for a honeymoon or family trip?
faqs.answer: This itinerary suits couples, solo travelers, and small groups equally well — talk to your consultant about tailoring it further.

reviews.author: Hiroshi Sharma
reviews.title: Smooth from start to finish
reviews.body: Neon Museum alone was worth the trip, but combined with Cirque du Soleil "O" (Bellagio) this itinerary really delivered. Breakfast at the hotel was good every day and the local guide was knowledgeable.

reviews.author: Arjun Novak
reviews.title: Solid mid-range package
reviews.body: Booked this as a couple trip and it was well paced. Mob Museum in particular stood out. A couple of the transfers ran a little late but nothing that spoiled the trip.

reviews.author: Wei Johnson
reviews.title: Solid mid-range package
reviews.body: Booked this as a family of 4 trip and it was well paced. Mob Museum in particular stood out. A couple of the transfers ran a little late but nothing that spoiled the trip.

reviews.author: Lukas Dubois
reviews.title: Solid mid-range package
reviews.body: Las Vegas is stunning and this package covered the essentials well, especially Welcome to Fabulous Las Vegas sign. The hotel could have been a little closer to the center but transport was easy.

reviews.author: Ethan Smith
reviews.title: Great value for money
reviews.body: Everything was handled for us — flights, hotel, transfers, and tours to The Venetian canal walk and Cirque du Soleil "O" (Bellagio). Great communication from the consultant before departure too.

reviews.author: Noah Fernandez
reviews.title: Exceeded our expectations
reviews.body: Our trip to Las Vegas was fantastic from start to finish. The hotel was clean and well located, and the guided visit to Titanic Exhibition (Luxor) was a real highlight. Airport transfers were on time both ways.

reviews.author: Tom Santos
reviews.title: So many hidden gems included
reviews.body: We added a couple of optional activities beyond The Sphere show and don't regret it. Good value for what was included, and the food recommendations from our guide were spot on.

reviews.author: Emma Rossi
reviews.title: Great value for money
reviews.body: Our trip to Las Vegas was fantastic from start to finish. The hotel was clean and well located, and the guided visit to Hoover Dam guided tour was a real highlight. Airport transfers were on time both ways.
